topic: Solar elevation from H(r) — verification of EXP-330 patch (direct-import-with-form-substitution rebuttal)
change_summary: EXP-330 replaced the generic post-hoc rider in WIN-056.detail_verdict_text (973 chars) with a WIN-056-specific paragraph (2,566 chars) naming the failure mode 'direct parameter import with form-substitution' and pinning the evidence to raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 25 + Duffie & Beckman 1980. Opening sentence and Part 3.1b/dome-author-definition closing preserved verbatim. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) unchanged. detail_evidence (2,095 chars), claim, finding, tldrs, and code_analysis tags all unchanged.

our_argument_summary: The patched detail_verdict_text replaces the generic post-hoc retrodiction rider with a WIN-056-specific argument naming the failure mode 'direct parameter import with form-substitution.' The argument: (a) the dome had the right theoretical posture in building H(r), (b) but never closed the loop — it substituted the textbook globe formula φ_sun(t) = 23.45° × sin(2π(t−81)/365) verbatim, (c) 23.45° is Earth's J2000.0 axial obliquity (23.4393°), treated as a direct numerical input in the V9 coordinate system (raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 25), (d) the sinusoidal form is standard solar engineering (Duffie & Beckman 1980), valid only for a tilted sphere orbiting a distant Sun, (e) the dome's own geometry would yield θ = arctan(H_sun/d_obs), not a sinusoid in day-of-year, (f) so the failure mode is stronger than ordinary retrodiction — it is wholesale transcription of the rival model's value, functional form, and geometric justification. The defender's 'every model needs inputs' objection is preempted and conceded with the caveat that wholesale import from the rival model is transcription, not prediction. The argument upgrades the original critique without softening the Self-Contradicted verdict.

relevant_files: raw-text/05-model.txt (Sun altitude 5,733 km optical / <4,200 km physical, line 38; H(r) function)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: The dome publishes φ_sun(t) = 23.45° × sin(2π(t − 81)/365) on its solar elevation derivation page, importing the value 23.45° and the sinusoidal day-of-year functional form from the globe model. No derivation from disc_radius (20,015 km), H(r) = 8537 × exp(−r/8619), sun_altitude (5,733 km), or any dome geometry parameter is provided. The V9 coordinate system independently uses 23.44° (the same constant) as a direct numerical input at line 25 — confirming that the dome's own internal toolchain treats this value as imported, not derived.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 23.44° hardcoded at raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 25; 23.45° appears in the dome's solar elevation formula as the amplitude — both treated as direct numerical inputs, neither computed from dome parameters. The new detail_verdict_text correctly names both values and pins them to J2000.0 obliquity (23.4393°).
data_sources_used: raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (dome's own V9 coordinates page extraction)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None new in the patched text. Aetheric refraction (the dome's typical out for elevation/parallax/refraction WINs) is not invoked in the patched paragraph; the patched argument is purely about formula provenance and is independent of refraction handwaves. The text explicitly invites the dome to publish a derivation: 'The dome could close the loop by specifying sun motion within the cavity, deriving the resulting elevation-vs-latitude curve from dome parameters alone, and computing the sinusoidal amplitude (if any) without reference to 23.45°.'
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. The patched text's claims about raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 25 are independently verified by grep against the raw-text archive. The J2000.0 obliquity value 23.4393° is consistent with IAU 2006 (23.4392811° at J2000.0), rounded appropriately.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c2. WIN-056 is a static geometric claim. No monitor.py domain tracks the solar elevation formula. The dome's coordinate system uses 23.44° as a constant. The patch did not introduce a new monitoring pathway.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c2. The sinusoidal solar declination formula δ(t) = 23.45° × sin(2π(t−81)/365) is standard solar engineering (Cooper 1969, Duffie & Beckman 1980). The dome adopts the formula verbatim and relabels its origin from 'Earth's axial tilt' to 'dome H(r) geometry.' The patched detail_verdict_text now states this relabeling explicitly with citation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c2. The sinusoidal declination formula was textbook ~46 years before WIN-056 was claimed in V51.0 (April 2026). The patched text now anchors this with the Duffie & Beckman 1980 citation.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c2. The formula φ_sun(t) = 23.45° × sin(2π(t−81)/365) contains no dome-specific parameters; it does not depend on disc_radius, H(r), sun_altitude, or any dome geometry. The patched text strengthens this with the explicit V9 coordinate system anchor (lat_S = 90 − noon_altitude_june + 23.44 at raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 25) and names the failure mode as 'direct parameter import' rather than the weaker 'no derivation provided.'
code_analysis_tags.cycle_3_changes_from_c2: All four tags unchanged. The patch did not change the underlying classification — it tightened the prose articulation of the same failure mode that c2 already documented (formula_substitution_confirmed=true).

holes_found.description: The patched text cites Duffie & Beckman 1980 as the source of the sinusoidal solar declination approximation. Duffie & Beckman (Solar Engineering of Thermal Processes, 1st ed. 1980) is the standard textbook reference, but the actual origin of the simple sinusoidal form δ(t) = 23.45° × sin(...) is Cooper (1969, 'The absorption of radiation in solar stills,' Solar Energy 12:333-346). A pedant dome defender could note that Duffie & Beckman cite Cooper, not invent the formula. The current phrasing 'standard solar engineering (Duffie & Beckman 1980, ~46 years before WIN-056 was claimed)' is not wrong — it describes the formula as appearing in their textbook, which it does — but pinning the formula's first appearance would more accurately reference Cooper 1969.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional refinement (not required): either replace 'Duffie & Beckman 1980' with 'Cooper 1969 / Duffie & Beckman 1980' or rephrase as 'standard textbook solar engineering (see Duffie & Beckman 1980, Solar Engineering of Thermal Processes)' to make clear the citation is to the canonical textbook, not the originator. The 46-year framing still works either way (Cooper 1969 → 2026 is 57 years; Duffie & Beckman 1980 → 2026 is 46 years). Severity is minor because the substantive argument is unaffected and Duffie & Beckman is the legitimately citable canonical reference.
holes_found.severity_rationale: Pedantic citation precision. Does not affect verdict, does not give a defender a real opening, does not require a patch.

holes_found.description: The patched text says 'the dome's own geometry produces θ = arctan(H_sun/d_obs), an arctangent in observer distance, not a sinusoid in day-of-year.' This is correct at any fixed instant for an observer at horizontal distance d_obs from the sub-solar point. However, a dome defender could argue that if the sun moves on a circular path within the cavity (which the dome model does posit at varying r_sun(t)), the observer's elevation over time IS a function of time — just not the simple 23.45° sinusoid. The text's strong phrasing 'not a sinusoid in day-of-year' could be read as denying any time-dependence, when the actual argument is that the SPECIFIC 23.45°-amplitude sinusoid cannot emerge without invoking axial tilt.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional refinement: 'an arctangent in observer distance, not the 23.45°-amplitude sinusoid in day-of-year that the globe formula provides' would make the contrast more precise. As currently written, the simpler 'not a sinusoid' phrasing is defensible because the dome's own derivation would have to produce a 23.45° amplitude in time, which dome geometry alone cannot do without specifying sun motion AND showing that motion happens to yield exactly 23.45° amplitude. The c2 review (SA-002) already established this rigorously.
holes_found.severity_rationale: Imprecision that doesn't mislead. The c2 deeper_analysis already covers the nuance: 'the specific amplitude of 23.45° would need to emerge from dome parameters (H₀=8537, λ=8619, H_sun=5733, etc.), not be inserted as a constant.' The new text is a shorter restatement and the spirit is correct.

stronger_arguments.description: The patched text's framing 'direct parameter import with form-substitution' could be sharpened by explicitly listing the three components imported wholesale from the globe: (1) the numerical constant 23.45° (Earth's obliquity), (2) the functional form sin(2π(t − 81)/365) (textbook spring-equinox-referenced sinusoid), (3) the geometric justification (axial tilt of a spinning sphere relative to its orbital plane around a distant Sun). The text does name all three but slightly diffusely — a structured 'three components, all imported' framing would make the upgrade vs. retrodiction more visible. The current text says 'the globe's 23.45°, the globe's sinusoidal functional form, and the globe's geometric justification' which is close to this — the refinement would just be making the list more visually distinct (parenthetical, em-dash, or numbered).
stronger_arguments.action_recommended: Optional. Current text is already clear. This is a polish suggestion, not a hole.

kernel_of_truth.description: The patched detail_verdict_text correctly acknowledges the dome's kernel: the H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) construction unifies three independent firmament-height measurements (Schumann ~9,500 km, Polaris ~4,750 km, model-average ~9,086 km) with an exponential decay, and the theoretical posture (derive observables from internal parameters) is the right posture for a self-consistent model (see EXP-330 kernel_of_truth field and analyst.md kernel-of-truth standard).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Acknowledging the kernel does not save the claim because, having built H(r), the dome did not derive φ_sun(t) from it — it substituted the globe formula verbatim. The patched text makes this explicit: 'The execution, however, never closes the loop.' The kernel is bounded (sophistication of curve-fitting, correct theoretical posture) and the takedown follows immediately. This is the structure the kernel-of-truth standard is designed to produce: praise the framework, show the specific point at which the framework was abandoned for borrowed answers.

cross_win_consistency.win_057_template_misapplication_lineage: WIN-057.c3 was patched via EXP-329 for the same template-misapplication issue (generic post-hoc rider that did not match the actual failure mode). EXP-330 replicates the WIN-057 fix on WIN-056. The cross-WIN consistency review in WIN-057.c3.json explicitly flagged WIN-056 as a sibling. EXP-330 closes the WIN-056 instance of that audit.
cross_win_consistency.remaining_template_carriers: WIN-012 (mag-gravity coupling 1.67), WIN-030 (elliptical disc geometry), WIN-069 (Australia road scaffold) — the generic phrase 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters' still appears in these three WINs. Per EXP-330 summary_for_decider and CLAUDE.md cross-WIN consistency discipline, EACH should be inspected on its own merits — for these WINs the rider may legitimately fit (they involve values fit to globe data via dome-internal computation), and a mechanical sweep is explicitly NOT warranted. This audit is NOT in scope for WIN-056.c3.
cross_win_consistency.cross_win_recommendation: Decider should queue WIN-012/030/069 individually for analyst per-WIN review (NOT a single sweeping EXP), if and when their templates are confirmed misapplied. The EXP-330 author explicitly flagged this in their summary_for_decider.

reasoning_for_recommendation: EXP-330 PATCH-A integrated cleanly into the FUSE workspace. All 7 verification anchors specified in the EXP's patches[0].verification pass. Structural preservation (opening sentence, blank-line separator, Part 3.1b cross-reference, dome-author-definition link, verdict, claim, finding, tldrs, code_analysis tags, detail_evidence) is fully preserved. The new argument 'direct parameter import with form-substitution' survives adversarial review — the J2000.0 obliquity claim (23.4393°) is correct, the V9 coordinate anchor (raw-text/04-coordinates.txt line 25) is verified, the Duffie & Beckman 1980 reference is legitimate (with the minor pedantic note that Cooper 1969 is the formula's origin — a minor severity citation issue not requiring a patch), the arctan vs sinusoid contrast is mathematically correct, and the 'every model needs inputs' counter is preempted. The kernel-of-truth acknowledgement (H(r) construction sophistication) is appropriately bounded by the immediately following 'the execution, however, never closes the loop.' No verdict change, no major refinement needed. The two minor findings (citation precision, mathematical phrasing nuance) are optional refinements, not required patches. Confidence increased from c2's 0.96 to 0.97 because the patch closes the specific weakness c2 flagged in H-003 (generic-template framing).

deeper_analysis:
This is a verification-class re-review (PROP-025 class='verification'), triggered by the decider's EXP-330 integration and queue push. The decider classification is correct: EXP-330 is a tight, single-WIN prose patch with no verdict change and no new monitoring/data introduced — exactly the verification-class profile. The patch lands cleanly and the new argument is stronger than what it replaces. The argument upgrade from 'post-hoc retrodiction' to 'direct parameter import with form-substitution' is substantive — retrodiction (fit parameters after seeing data) is a weaker indictment than transcription (take the entire formula whole, including the foreign physics justification). The new framing also gives the reader a clearer mental model of how to recognize the same failure mode in future WINs.

The one operationally-important note: EXP-330 surfaces a cross-WIN audit obligation for WIN-012/030/069 (the three other WINs that still carry the generic-template phrase). EXP-330 author correctly scoped these out of the current patch and flagged them for separate per-WIN inspection. The decider should NOT treat EXP-330 as setting precedent for a mechanical sweep — the analyst's caution is appropriate, and the curmudgeon endorses the per-WIN approach. WIN-012 in particular involves the 1.67 mag-gravity coupling that IS computed from observed eclipse data (a different failure mode — assembly-from-fits, not raw-import), so the generic rider may legitimately fit. WIN-030 elliptical geometry and WIN-069 road scaffold also have their own profiles. Decider: queue these three WINs for analyst review one at a time, not as a batch.

A smart dome defender's strongest line against the patched text: 'You concede H(r) is sophisticated. If the dome posits a sun motion model that, combined with H(r), naturally produces a 23.45°-amplitude apparent elevation pattern, your critique evaporates.' Our response (the patched text already provides this): 'The dome COULD close the loop by specifying sun motion within the cavity... Until that derivation exists, WIN-056 is a relabeling, not a derivation.' This is the right rebuttal — the burden is on the dome to publish the derivation, not on us to imagine it. The patched text places this burden explicitly without making us look uncharitable.

The FUSE/git divergence at review time is worth flagging: the patch is in the FUSE workspace but not yet in git origin/main. Universal-pusher workspace-sync rescues this within an hour. The build pipeline reads from the clone, so until the next workspace-sync push, builds would render the pre-patch text. The decider's run that self-applied EXP-330 should have either committed-and-pushed (the canonical path) or marked the file for FUSE-rescue (universal-pusher path). If this divergence persists past the next workspace-sync cycle, tinker should investigate. For curmudgeon purposes, the FUSE state is authoritative for the content under review.

citations_verified.verification: Verified via grep: 'lat_S = 90 - noon_altitude_june + 23.44' present at line 25 of the V9 reference section. The dome's own internal coordinate toolchain treats 23.44° as a direct numerical input.

Consistent with IAU 2006 obliquity model (23.4392811° at J2000.0 epoch), rounded to 4 sig figs. The dome's 23.44° (in V9) and 23.45° (in solar elevation formula) both round to this value — confirming the parameter is Earth's axial obliquity, not a coincidence.

citations_verified.source: Duffie & Beckman, Solar Engineering of Thermal Processes (1980)
citations_verified.verification: Real book, 1st edition 1980, canonical reference for solar engineering. Contains the standard sinusoidal solar declination approximation. Legitimate citation. Note: the formula's actual origin is Cooper 1969 — Duffie & Beckman cite Cooper, so the patched text's framing 'standard solar engineering (Duffie & Beckman 1980)' is correct as a canonical textbook attribution, not a first-publication claim.

citations_unchecked.source: Dome's live V51.0 derivation page for WIN-056
citations_unchecked.reason: Carried over from c2 — V51.0 raw-text not extracted; the patch's claims about the dome's published formula rely on the V9 coordinate evidence (verified) and the formula's appearance in dome's predictions/model pages (inferred from polling data 'V50.10 introduces solar elevation, 58 confirmed wins'). The verdict does not depend on quote verification of the V51.0 page text. Optional: future polling cycle could extract the V51.0 WIN-056 page for the archive.

summary_for_decider:
VERIFICATION-CLASS REVIEW — EXP-330 PATCH-A integrated cleanly. The new 'direct parameter import with form-substitution' framing is a genuine argument upgrade over the generic post-hoc rider it replaces, and it survives adversarial review. All 7 verification anchors pass. Verdict (Self-Contradicted) holds; no verdict change recommended. Two minor findings (citation precision for Cooper 1969 vs Duffie & Beckman 1980; phrasing nuance on 'not a sinusoid in day-of-year') are optional refinements, not blocking. NO new ISS recommended. NO carry-over from c2 (this is a verification of a patch, not a holistic re-review; c2's outstanding editorial holes ISS-370/H-001..H-006 are separately tracked and out of scope here).

CROSS-WIN HEADS-UP: EXP-330 surfaces a cross-WIN audit obligation for WIN-012/030/069 (other generic-template carriers). EXP-330 author correctly scoped these out. Decider should queue these three WINs one at a time for analyst per-WIN review (NOT a single sweeping EXP); the template may legitimately fit some and not others. WIN-012 likely involves a different failure mode (assembly-from-eclipse-fits). Do not let EXP-330 set precedent for a mechanical sweep.

OPERATIONAL NOTE: EXP-330 patch is in FUSE workspace but not in git origin/main at the time of this review. Universal-pusher workspace-sync should rescue within ~1h. If the divergence persists past the next workspace-sync cycle (~07:06Z), flag for tinker investigation. The build pipeline reads from the clone, so until rescue, the live site renders pre-patch text. The decider's run that self-applied EXP-330 should have committed-and-pushed; if it did not, this is a soft pipeline bug to surface.
